label: The Blackwrit Causeway
description: The Blackwrit Causeway is a shattered stone embankment spanning a drowned lowland, its surviving arches stamped with weathered writ-seals and boundary marks from a defunct charter regime. Collapsed parapets expose rebar-like iron cramps and pockets of fused slag, enduring evidence of the continent-scale catastrophe that broke Muraii’s trade corridors. Tide-stained plinths and toppled mile-stones divide the route into numbered spans, each bearing chisel-cut interdiction glyphs that designate codified hazard zones. The air carries a persistent brine and ash tang, and the surrounding shallows remain littered with drowned masonry that constrains passage to the remaining intact spans.
